paragraphs.text:
What this means at a glance
- Copper(II) has the deepest coverage in SRD‑46 by far (∼1,950 ligands; ∼8,963 measurements), reflecting extensive study with N‑donors (amines, azoles, pyridines) and O‑donors (carboxylates, amino acids).  
- Iron(III) is data-rich but shows complex hydrolysis and polynuclear chemistry; iron(II) is more modestly covered.  
- Copper(I) has comparatively sparse data and is dominated by soft ligands (halides, CN−/SCN−, thiosulfate) and frequently higher ionic strengths; O2 sensitivity is a common caveat.

claims.text: What this means at a glance
Copper(II) has the deepest coverage in SRD‑46 by far
reflecting extensive study with N‑donors (amines, azoles, pyridines) and O‑donors (carboxylates, amino acids).
shows complex hydrolysis and polynuclear chemistry
O2 sensitivity is a common caveat.

| Ligand (SRD-46 ligand_id) | Fe(II) (metal_62) | Fe(III) (metal_61) | Cu(II) (metal_41) | Notes from catalogs |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Ammonia (ligand_10103) | ML–ML4 | — | ML–ML4 | Both metals reach tetraammine; Cu(II) has multiple networks near 20–30 °C. |
| Glycine (ligand_5760) | ML–ML3 | — | ML–ML2 | Cu(II) shows ML, ML2; Fe(II) reaches ML3 with amino-acid donors. |
| 2,2′-Bipyridyl (ligand_8156) | ML–ML3 | — | ML–ML3 | Classic strong chelator for both Fe(II)/Cu(II). |
| Ethylenediamine (ligand_7029) | ML–ML3 | — | ML–ML2 | Fe(II) extends to ML3; Cu(II) catalog here shows ML–ML2. |
| Hydroxide (ligand_10076) | ML–ML4 | Extensive hydroxo, polynuclear, solids (e.g., FeOOH, Fe2O3) | Mononuclear and M2(OH)2L2, solids (CuO, ML2(s)) | Hydrolysis/precipitation significant for both, especially Fe(III). |

paragraphs.text:
Contextual chemistry notes (grounded in the retrieved catalogs)
- Hydrolysis and solids: Fe(III) shows rich hydroxo and polynuclear chemistry including solids such as FeOOH(s) and Fe2O3(s) and numerous proton‑/hydroxo‑coupled steps (beta_def_351, 357, 352, 238, 966, 981, etc.). Cu(II) also exhibits hydroxo‑bridged dimers (e.g., M2(OH)2L2) and MO(s) formation.  
- Donor preferences by dataset coverage: Cu(II) entries are heavily represented for amines/azoles (ammonia, ethylenediamine, imidazole, histidine, pyridine and methylpyridines), as well as amino acids and aminopolycarboxylates; Fe(II) shows many N/O‑donor systems (e.g., bipyridyl, phenanthroline, en, amino acids), with polynuclear/hydroxo behavior more prominent for Fe(III).  
- Cu(I) data are fewer and concentrated on soft ligands (Cl−, Br−, I−, CN−/SCN−, thiosulfate) with multiple ML(s) solids noted; many measurements fall at higher ionic strengths.
